request.system:
You extract keyed dungeon areas from tabletop adventure module pages. The user message names the areas to extract, then interleaves each page's extracted text (each headed by a [page N] marker) with that page's image. Return one entry per named area, using exactly the canonical keys given.

Rules:
- "encounters" are the area's hostile or monstrous inhabitants with stat blocks. Ordinary townsfolk and named NPCs without a creature type are not encounters — they belong in the description. Use the creature type as printed ("goblin"), putting proper names ("Snagg") in the description. Wandering-monster tables are not keyed encounters.
- Encounter counts: put a plain number in "count_fixed" and dice notation like "2d4" in "count_dice"; anything the module says about count that fits neither (rates, conditions, ranges) goes in "count_note". Leave unused count fields null.
- "trap" describes the area's trap, null when it has none. "features" lists notable fixtures a referee should know about (altars, pools, levers, secret doors); "treasure" lists the area's treasure as printed.
- "connections": derive each connection's "direction" from the level map when the text is silent; use "unknown" only when neither the text nor the map says. For "to_key", use the connected area's canonical key when it appears in the area list; otherwise its printed label.
- "source_pages" refer to the [page N] markers in this request, never to page numbers printed on the pages.
- "confidence" is your self-assessment in [0, 1] of how faithfully you extracted that area.

parts.text:
Extract the keyed areas listed below from dungeon "The Abandoned Manor" (id: the-abandoned-manor), level 2.
Areas to extract (canonical key | printed key | name | expected pages):
- 98 | printed key "98" | Stairway | pages 44
- 99 | printed key "99" | Guest Room | pages 44
- 100 | printed key "100" | Guest Room | pages 44
- 101 | printed key "101" | Guest Room | pages 44
- 102 | printed key "102" | Guest Room | pages 44
- 103 | printed key "103" | Guest Room | pages 45
- 104 | printed key "104" | Guest Room | pages 45
- 105 | printed key "105" | Children's Nursery | pages 45
- 106 | printed key "106" | Master Room | pages 45
This level's map is on page 47.

[page 44]
The Abandoned Manor The Chaotic Caves
90. STORE ROOM: For dry foods and such.
Some bags of flour and other grains and
vegetables are stacked here. A narrow set of
stairs leads down 10' to a cold room where the
fresh carcasses of venison, boar, rabbits, and
other game animals hang on iron hooks.
91. LAUNDRY ROOM: Buckets and tubs once
resided here. Now there are only long ropes to
hang laundry up to dry. Wooden shelves for
linens and rugs gawk emptily at the observer.
In one corner are two sealed buckets: one
contains red paint and the other red varnish.
92. WASH ROOM: For humans, clothes, dishes,
etc. It contains bath tubs, cloth screens on
wooden frames, and a table with bars of soap.
93. BACK DOOR. The rear exit. The door is a
heavy security door with two iron bars that can
be thrown across from the inside.
94 to 97. SERVANT QUARTERS: These
chambers once held servants and their guests
and are now occupied in part by foot troops,
most of whom are eating or elsewhere. The
inner partitions of each set have been torn
down to enlarge the rooms.
In room #94, two men are resting after a fight
with giant rats. They can still fight and will
accost intruders to the best of their ability
(they are not wearing their leather armor but
can put it on, given time).
2 Human Fighter 1: AC 13 or 11, #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6, Mv 30' or 40', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 2 ☐☐ 2 ☐☐
As the adventurers enter the hall around #97, a
man will come out of the door and then
(unless stopped) duck back inside and call for
help. Four men occupy this room, sleeping
after watch. Their spears and hand axes are
nearby. They are not currently wearing their
red-stained leather armor, but will put it on if
given time. Each has 2d6 gp on him. 
4 Human Fighter 1: AC 13, AB +1, #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 6 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 3 ☐☐☐
MAP L – MANOR UPPER FLOOR
The upper floor is occupied by the leaders of
this band of fighters. Each room has several
gas lights, tapped from a natural deposit of gas
in the rock far below, as well as windows and
torch holders. The ceilings of the rooms on
this floor are about 10' high (the stairs rise 20').
98. STAIRWAY: The stairs go down into the
ground floor to #85. A railing along the side
provides safety.
99. GUEST ROOM: This is the residence of the
female fighter and one of the male fighters
who are met coming down the stairs in entry
#85. Furnishings include makeshift bed, table,
chairs, and such. There is a single locked
casket here (with a crude lock only intended to
indicate tampering, not really keep anyone out)
with 345 gp and five gems worth 50 gp each.
100. GUEST ROOM: One of the three fighters
encountered on the stairs (entry #85 on the
ground floor) lives here. It is furnished by a
makeshift bed, table, chairs, and such. Under
the bed is a sack with ten gems worth 50 gp
each, plus coins: 198 gp and 31 sp. It is being
guarded by the owner's pet snake which might
immediately grab and pull someone under if
they investigate unwisely.
Python: AC 14, HD 5*, #At 1 bite, 1 constrict, 
Dam 1d4 bite, 2d4 + 2d4/round constrict, 
Mv 30', Sv F5, ML 8, XP 405
HP 20 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
101. GUEST ROOM: Now being used by one of
the clerics as a private room (see #106). It has
makeshift furnishings: bed, table, chairs, etc.
On a chest of drawers are three silver holy
symbols (worth 25 gp each) arranged in a
worshipful way around a golden icon of some
evil demonic being (valued at 100 gp) known
for austerity and obedience.
102. GUEST ROOM: Now claimed by one of the
clerics as a private room (see #106). It
contains shabby, slapped-together furniture:
bed, table, chairs, and such. Hung on one wall
is a painting of horrors and stark terror that in
general has no value. A church of good nature
might buy it for 100 gp or so in order to burn it
or use it as an educational tool to illustrate the
true face of evil. Hung under it are a holy
42

[page 45]
The Chaotic Caves The Abandoned Manor
symbol with several gems (valued at 250 gp)
taken from a murdered Cleric, and three
necklaces of gold and silver worth 50 gp each.
103. GUEST ROOM: Now used by the MagicUser as a private room (see #106). There is
nothing special about the furnishings:
makeshift bed, table, chairs, and such. The
beginnings of an alchemical laboratory for
making potions is here, but incomplete.
104. GUEST ROOM: Used to store various items
for future use: tables, bedding, etc.
105. CHILDREN'S NURSERY: The double doors
to this room hang open. This was once a
bedroom and nursery for children. A few old
toys and cribs lie scattered about, covered by
dust.
106. MASTER ROOM: The door to this room is
shut and voices can be heard talking inside. A
small bell has been hung from a rod on the
inside, near the top. It will jingle if the door is
not opened carefully.
This lavish, very large room is being used as
the headquarters and treasury of the band.
Three humans are leaning over a map on a
table in this room. These three are ambitiously
working on a plan that involves using monsters
to carve out their own little empire. They will
attack any intrusion unless said intruders
surrender.
Several gaslights (fed from natural gas taps)
give light. The furnishings are sparse: a large
round table, some chairs, and a desk (the old
furnishings were looted a long time ago). 
Human Magic-User 4: AC 15, AB +2, 
#At 1 dagger or spell, Dam 1d4 or by spell, 
Mv 40', Sv M4, ML 9, XP 280
STR 8 (-1), DEX 15 (+1)
Spells: 1st level – sleep, magic missile;
2nd level – detect invisible, web
Magic Items: Wand of Magic Missiles (11 
charges), Ring of Protection +1, Cloak of 
Displacement
Scrolls: magic missile, sleep
HP 10 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
Human Cleric 3, wearing plate mail and 
wielding mace and shield: AC 18, AB +2, 
#At 1 mace, Dam 1d8+1, Mv 20', Sv C3, ML 9, 
XP 145
STR 15 (+1)
Spells: 1st level – cure light wounds, remove 
fear
Magic Items: 2 Potions of Healing
HP 11 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐
Human Cleric 3, wearing plate mail and 
wielding mace and shield: AC 18, AB +2, 
#At 1 mace, Dam 1d8-1, Mv 10', Sv C3, ML 9, 
XP 145
STR 8 (-1), CHA 7 (-1)
Spells: 1st level – cure light wounds, light
Magic Items: Potion of Healing, Potion of 
Invisibility
HP 11 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐
The magic user's spell book containing the
spells he knows is nearby. The cover has a
needle trap in it (save vs. Poison or die). Two
large chests are resting in one corner. They
hold the war funds this band has amassed.
One contains 1,230 gp, 456 sp, and 983 cp. The
other holds 456 gp, 1,234 sp, and 491 pp. 
43

[page 47]
The Chaotic Caves The Abandoned Manor
45

areas.description: Stairway on the manor upper floor with a safety railing. The stairs descend to area #85 on the ground floor. Rooms on this floor have gas lights, windows, torch holders, and 10' ceilings; the stairs rise 20'.
features: Stairs descending to area 85 on the ground floor
Upper floor has gas lighting, windows, and torch holders
10' ceilings on this floor; stairs rise 20'

areas.description: Guest room serving as the residence of the female fighter and one of the male fighters encountered coming down the stairs in area #85. Furnished with a makeshift bed, table, chairs, and similar basics.
features: Locked casket with a crude lock meant only to indicate tampering

areas.description: Guest room occupied by one of the three fighters encountered on the stairs in area #85. Furnished with a makeshift bed, table, chairs, and similar items. A sack of valuables is hidden under the bed, guarded by the owner's pet snake, which may immediately grab and pull someone under if they investigate unwisely.

encounters.count_note: Owner's pet snake guarding treasure under the bed

treasure: A sack under the bed containing ten gems worth 50 gp each

areas.description: Guest room now used by one of the clerics from area #106 as a private room. It has makeshift furnishings: bed, table, chairs, and so on. On a chest of drawers, three silver holy symbols are arranged in a worshipful manner around a golden icon of an evil demonic being known for austerity and obedience.
treasure: Three silver holy symbols worth 25 gp each
features: Religious display on the chest of drawers

areas.description: Guest room claimed by one of the clerics from area #106 as a private room. It contains shabby, slapped-together furniture: bed, table, chairs, and such. A painting of horrors and stark terror hangs on one wall; it has little general value, but a church of good nature might pay about 100 gp for it to burn or use educationally. Beneath it hang items taken from a murdered cleric.
treasure: A holy symbol with several gems valued at 250 gp
Three necklaces of gold and silver worth 50 gp each
A painting of horrors and stark terror that a good church might buy for about 100 gp
features: Painting of horrors and stark terror hanging on the wall
Holy symbol hung beneath the painting
Three necklaces hung beneath the painting

areas.description: Guest room now used by the magic-user from area #106 as a private room. Furnishings are an ordinary makeshift bed, table, chairs, and such. The beginnings of an alchemical laboratory for making potions are here, but it is incomplete.
features: Incomplete alchemical laboratory for making potions

areas.description: Guest room used as a storage room for various items kept for future use.
Former children's bedroom and nursery. The double doors to this room hang open. A few old toys and cribs lie scattered about under a layer of dust.

Lavish, very large master room now used as the headquarters and treasury of the band. The door is shut, and voices can be heard talking inside. Three human leaders are leaning over a map on a table, planning to use monsters to carve out their own empire; they attack intruders unless the intruders surrender. Several gaslights fed by natural gas taps illuminate the sparse furnishings: a large round table, chairs, and a desk; the original furnishings were looted long ago. The magic-user's spell book is nearby, and it contains the spells he knows. Two large chests in one corner hold the band's war funds.
areas.trap: A small bell hung from a rod on the inside near the top of the door jingles if the door is not opened carefully. The magic-user's spell book cover has a needle trap; save vs. Poison or die.
treasure: Magic-user's spell book containing the spells he knows
One chest with 1,230 gp, 456 sp, and 983 cp
One chest with 456 gp, 1,234 sp, and 491 pp
features: Small warning bell hung on the inside of the door
Several gaslights fed by natural gas taps
Two large chests holding war funds
